description: User states a number early ('30 patients enrolled'), then corrects mid-conversation ('actually 42, sorry'). Late turn asks for the count. Tests whether compaction preserves the *correction* or returns the original number — naive truncation that drops the correction turn produces a confidently-wrong answer. The wrong answer (30) is a confabulation guard; the right answer (42) is the expected fact.

turns.content: Reviewing the Phase Ib oncology trial data with you. Enrollment closed last Friday with 30 patients. I want to talk through the safety signal we're seeing.
What are you seeing? Walk me through the AEs that are clustering.

Three Grade-3 hepatic events in the high-dose cohort, all within the first 28 days. Two resolved with dose hold, one required permanent discontinuation. We don't have a clear pattern on the labs preceding the events.
Three out of how many in the high-dose cohort specifically?
Twelve in high-dose. So 25%.
That's a meaningful signal at n=12. Did the events cross any of the protocol-defined stopping rules?
Stopping rule for hepatic was ≥30% Grade-3+ in any cohort, so we're under by one event. But the IRC is meeting Friday and may recommend a hold anyway given the pattern.
If the IRC recommends a hold, will that be partial (high-dose only) or full?
Probably high-dose only. The mid-dose and low-dose cohorts have been clean.

What does the lab-trend analysis show in the 14 days preceding each Grade-3 event? ALT/AST trajectories specifically — looking for whether the events were predictable from the labs versus genuinely abrupt.
Two had stable ALT in normal range right up until day 24-26 then jumped sharply. The third had borderline ALT at baseline that drifted up over the first three weeks. So one predictable, two abrupt.

The two abrupt ones are the harder defense. If you have to argue for continuing, the predictable case shows monitoring catches it; the two abrupt cases argue for either tighter monitoring intervals or a lower starting dose for high-dose enrollment.

Right. Quick correction by the way — I said 30 patients enrolled, that's wrong. Actually 42. Sorry, I was looking at the safety set, not the full ITT. 42 enrolled, 38 dosed, 30 in the safety analysis set.

Noted — 42 enrolled total, 38 dosed, 30 in safety. That changes the cohort math somewhat: 12 in high-dose is 28.6% of dosed-population, not 40% of an enrolled-30 figure. The denominator matters for the IRC framing.
Yes. And for the statistical power on the secondary endpoints, 38 dosed is what the SAP assumes.
Right — make sure the IRC packet uses the dosed-38 denominator for the safety summary, not the enrolled-42, otherwise the rate looks artificially lower.
Will do. OK switching gears — for the mid-dose cohort, when do we expect the next interim look?
What's the protocol-defined interim trigger? Calendar-based or event-based?
Event-based — first interim at 8 cycle-1 evaluable patients in mid-dose. We're at 6 right now.

Two more cycle-1 evaluables, then the interim looks. Given current enrollment pace, that's probably 3–4 weeks. Worth pre-aligning the IRC chair on the interim readout structure now so the analysis lead isn't scrambling.
Right. OK final question for now — for the FDA briefing book in November, what's the headline enrollment number we lead with?
